principles: Icons are semantic visual anchors, not inline text decoration.
Prefer a separate data-pptx-type="icon" element with its own absolute box.
Do not place data-pptx-icon on textboxes except for legacy compatibility.
Text and icon regions must be separate rectangles with at least 8px clear gap.
HTML preview is the source of truth; PPTX must map the same icon element geometry.

placements.description: Icon occupies a fixed left gutter; title/body starts to the right.
Icon sits in its own top-left icon row; title begins below or to the right.
Small semantic badge in the top-right corner, separate from copy.
Metric card uses an icon column at left and value column at right.
Icon marks the metric theme above the number without sharing the text box.
Large low-density symbol supporting the cover thesis.
Low-opacity background symbol; never overlaps title readability.
Small marker paired with section kicker in a dedicated box.
Icon is the node marker on a timeline, label sits in a separate card.
Each process step reserves a header icon cell.
Icon inside an independent circular step marker.
Icon marks a connector transition or gate; copy remains in adjacent boxes.
Insight/caution callout reserves a left icon lane.
Badge icon sits outside the callout text block but inside the callout container.
Diagram node uses icon above label as two stacked boxes.
Diagram node uses icon cell and label cell side by side.
Each compared entity gets a consistent icon marker box.
Small icon tags a proof source or case evidence row.
Risk/warning icon sits in a reserved alert zone.
Opportunity/growth icon sits in a reserved opportunity zone.
Optional icon in a table header rail, not inside table cells.
Icon marks a chart annotation callout with a separate label.
Sidebar navigation/icon rail separate from main copy.
Large semantic anchor in whitespace, used sparingly to balance composition.
